channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: U.S. imposes sanctions on 35 individuals, entities for aiding Iran's sanctions evasions
item.description: The US imposed sanctions on 35 individuals and entities for aiding Iran's sanctions evasions to disrupt Tehran's payment mechanisms. The designations were intended to expose and disrupt the mechanisms used by Tehran to receive payment for oil and other commodities and reduce its revenue, Treasury said.

item.title: U.K. PM Keir Starmer wins crunch Parliament vote over ethics inquiry
item.description: UK PM Keir Starmer won a Parliament vote, 335 to 223, against an ethics inquiry into his statements on Peter Mandelson's appointment. Members of Parliament voted 335 to 223 against a Privileges Committee investigation into whether he had misled the House of Commons over the appointment of disgraced peer Peter Mandelson.

item.title: S Jaishankar Meets United Nations General Assembly Chief, Discusses Global Challenges
item.description: S Jaishankar met with Annalena Baerbock to discuss global challenges as President of the United Nations General Assembly. S Jaishankar with Annalena Baerbock, President of the United Nations General Assembly.

item.title: U.S. Marines board merchant ship in Arabian Sea suspected to be headed to Iran
item.description: U. S. Marines boarded a merchant ship in the Arabian Sea, suspected of heading to Iran, but released it after a search confirmed no Iranian port call. The incident occurred without incident, with CENTCOM stating the ship was allowed to continue its voyage.

item.title: US appeals court rejects Trump’s immigration detention policy
item.description: A US appeals court rejected Trump's immigration detention policy in a 3-0 ruling, saying it misread a decades-old law. In a 3-0 ruling, court says Trump administration misread a decades-old immigration law to justify mandatory detention.

item.title: US to produce passports featuring Trump's image
item.description: The US will produce limited edition passports featuring Donald Trump's image to commemorate the country's 250th year of independence. Donald Trump's picture will soon appear in limited edition passports, commemorating the United States' 250th year of independence.

item.title: Australia Proposes To Tax Meta, Google, TikTok To Fund Journalists
item.description: Australia proposes taxing Meta, Google, and TikTok to fund journalists, expecting to raise $144-179 million annually. The move aims to support the local journalism industry. Australia government expects the incentive would raise $144 million-$179 million a year.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran War News Highlights: Eight killed as Israel attacks south Lebanon despite ceasefire
item.description: Israel attacked south Lebanon, killing eight, despite a ceasefire. The incident occurred amid heightened tensions between Israel and Iran. US-Israel-Iran War News Highlights: Eight killed as Israel attacks south Lebanon despite ceasefire The Times of India.

item.title: NASA's Artemis II moonship returns home to its launch site after historic voyage
item.description: NASA's Artemis II moonship returned to its launch site after a historic voyage. The mission marked a significant milestone in the US space program. NASA's Artemis II moonship returns home to its launch site after historic voyage.

item.title: Washington-funded network's focus seen as fueling instability
item.description: A Washington-funded network's focus has been criticized for fueling instability. The network's activities have raised concerns in China. Washington-funded network's focus seen as fueling instability China Daily.

item.title: Balancing Asia’s energy transition in a high-demand era
item.description: Mitsubishi Power Asia Pacific CEO Akihiro Ondo emphasized the need for a pragmatic approach to balance energy reliability, affordability, and decarbonization in Asia. The region's rising power demand requires a system-wide solution.

item.title: Panama Canal Sees Spike In Vessel Traffic Due To War In Middle East
item.description: The Panama Canal Authority reports increased vessel traffic due to the US-Iran war in the Middle East, expecting more shipments to travel through the canal. This shift aims to avoid other conflict-affected routes.

item.title: Hardliners and moderates in Iran clash over talks with US: FT
item.description: Hardliners and moderates in Iran are clashing over potential talks with the US, according to the Financial Times. The divisions are emerging as Iran considers diplomatic engagement. Hardliners and moderates in Iran clash over talks with US: FT.

item.title: Trump's DOJ indicts former FBI director James Comey over '86 47' post
item.description: The US Department of Justice, under Trump, has indicted former FBI director James Comey over a 2017 social media post. The post contained a cryptic message that prosecutors argue was threatening.

item.title: Comey charged with threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
item.description: James Comey, former FBI director, has been charged by Trump's DOJ for allegedly threatening Trump's life in a 2025 Instagram post. The post featured a seashell photo. The new case stems from a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director that the justice department says calls for violence against Trump.

item.title: If Iran had nuclear weapon, whole world would be hostage, Trump says
item.description: Donald Trump stated that if Iran had a nuclear weapon, the entire world would be held hostage. He made these comments in a recent public statement on Iran's nuclear capabilities.

item.title: Trump says Tehran wants end to blockade; Israel kills medics
item.description: US President Trump said Iran has asked Washington to lift its naval blockade on Iranian ports, but the statement was made as Israeli forces killed several Palestinian medics in Gaza.

item.title: Former Asu chef Ace Tan opens Asin on Carpenter Street with Asian tasting menu that changes with
item.description: Former Asu chef Ace Tan has launched a new restaurant, Asin, on Carpenter Street, offering an Asian tasting menu that changes seasonally and draws on local ingredients and culinary traditions.

item.title: Trump claims Iran 'just' informed U.S. it's in 'state of collapse'
item.description: Donald Trump claimed that Iran told the US it is in a "state of collapse". Ceasefire efforts appear stalled, with Iran resisting US attempts to dictate terms. Ceasefire efforts appears at a standstill, with Iran saying Washington could no longer dictate terms.

item.title: Israeli soldier shows Gaza’s Beit Hanoon completely flattened
item.description: An Israeli soldier's video shows northern Gaza's Beit Hanoon neighborhood completely flattened, with widespread destruction visible in the footage. A video filmed by an Israeli soldier shows widespread destruction in northern Gaza.
